items.q: What is the gender of 'el día' (day)?
items.why: Students rely on '-a = feminine'. Common exceptions: el día, el mapa, el problema, el sistema, el clima (many Greek-origin -ma words). These appear constantly so must be memorised.

items.q: How do you say 'I like coffee' (in general) in Spanish?
items.why: English drops the article for general likes; Spanish keeps it. For general preferences, use the DEFINITE article: me gusta EL chocolate, me gustan LOS libros.

items.q: How do you say 'I am going to eat' in Spanish (near future)?
items.why: Students forget the 'a'. The structure is IR (conjugated) + A + INFINITIVE. The 'a' is essential and links the conjugated ir to the action. Voy comer is ungrammatical.
The answer examiners want is: a la izquierda. “a la derecha” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How do you ask 'Where is the park?'
items.why: Correct answer: ¿Dónde está el parque?. “¿Dónde es el parque?” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: If you take 'la tercera calle', which number street is that?
items.why: Exact answer: 3. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
